title: Harrison Ford Just Turned 84 — Which Of His Iconic Characters Are You?
dek: The man just picked up a Lifetime Achievement Award and Emmy buzz for 'Shrinking' — so let's find out which Harrison Ford legend you actually are.

quiz.intro: No wrong answers here — just answer honestly, then go find your match's movie on Flickle.

questions.question: A friend calls with a wild, spontaneous plan for tonight. What's your gut reaction?

answers.text: I'm in, as long as there's a legendary artifact involved.
Depends what it pays — and I'm not doing anything corny.
Sure, though I'm not totally convinced I want to be here at all.
Honestly? I'd rather just stay home with my family.
The cockpit of a ship that's somehow always one repair from disaster.
A rain-soaked, neon city where nobody wants the job you're doing.
A CIA desk job that keeps getting interrupted by actual danger.
A police precinct — though a barn-raising works too.

questions.question: What's your leadership style when everything goes sideways?

answers.text: Keep to yourself. Trust no one, question everything.
Stay calm, analyze fast, act decisively.
Protect the vulnerable first, worry about the rulebook later.
Lead from the front, personally, no matter the risk.

questions.question: Be honest — what's your biggest flaw?

answers.text: Can't say no to duty, even when it costs you time at home.
Stubborn — you'd rather follow instinct than orders.
Puts everyone else first, even when it's not safe to.
Obsessively controlling, refuses to admit when you're wrong.

questions.question: Pick your proudest moment of triumph.

answers.text: Standing up to corrupt authority to protect someone innocent.
Personally taking back control from people who never expected you to fight.
Building something entirely your own, from absolutely nothing.
Surviving a total disaster with your sense of humor intact.
Someone who can handle a crisis right alongside me.
Someone who'll follow me anywhere, no questions asked.
A coworker I never expected to fall for mid-disaster.
Someone just as stubborn and adventurous as I am.

questions.question: Pick a tool you'd want in a crisis.

answers.text: Whatever I can build with my own two hands.
A machete I found lying around and hoped worked.
A bullwhip. Don't ask why it works, it just does.
A blaster — never leave home without it.

questions.question: What genre of movie do you belong in?

answers.text: Romantic adventure-comedy.
Pulpy globe-trotting adventure serial.
Swashbuckling space opera.
Moody neo-noir sci-fi.

questions.question: How do you actually handle a crisis in the moment?

answers.text: Improvise something wild and hope it holds together.
Talk your way out — and if that fails, shoot your way out.
Shut down the emotion and just get it done.
Analyze fast, then act without hesitation.

questions.question: Pick the legacy you'd want people to remember.

answers.text: "Never tell me the odds."
Someone who forced us to ask what makes us human.
Quiet duty, done without needing the credit.
Doing right, even when the system said otherwise.

results.name: Indiana Jones
results.subtitle: Raiders of the Lost Ark (1981)
results.description: You're the reluctant hero who'd rather be lecturing but can't resist chasing a legend across the globe. Whip-smart, allergic to snakes, and somehow always one step ahead of a rolling boulder or a double-crossing rival.

results.name: Han Solo
results.subtitle: Star Wars: Episode IV - A New Hope (1977)
results.description: You mask genuine loyalty behind swagger, sarcasm, and a blaster you swear you'll only use if you have to. You'll deny caring about anyone right up until you're flying back in at the last possible second to save them.

results.name: Rick Deckard
results.subtitle: Blade Runner (1982)
results.description: You're weary, morally conflicted, and questioning everything — including whether you're even on the right side of the job. You do what needs doing because someone has to, even when it costs you certainty about who you really are.

results.name: Jack Ryan
results.subtitle: Patriot Games (1992)
results.description: You tried to live a quiet analyst's life, but duty and danger keep finding you anyway. Family always comes first, but when it counts, you're the calm, capable one everyone ends up needing.

results.name: John Book
results.subtitle: Witness (1985)
results.description: You're the big-city cop who ends up out of your element in a world with its own rules — and you end up learning as much as you protect. Principled to a fault, even when it costs you everything back home.

results.name: President James Marshall
results.subtitle: Air Force One (1997)
results.description: You're the leader who used to be a soldier first — calm under fire and personally hands-on when it matters most. You're famous for one very specific line about hijackers and your plane.

results.name: Allie Fox
results.subtitle: The Mosquito Coast (1986)
results.description: You're the brilliant, stubborn inventor convinced modern civilization is doomed, dragging everyone you love into the jungle to build a better world on your own terms. Charismatic right up until it tips into dangerous.

results.name: Quinn Harris
results.subtitle: Six Days Seven Nights (1998)
results.description: You handle total disaster with sarcasm, competence, and zero patience for anyone's nonsense — including your own. An everyday pilot thrown into a survival-romance you never signed up for, but you always land the plane.
